title: Cache Performance: Hit Ratio and Access Time
summary: Defines hit/miss ratio, derives the average memory access time formula, and analyses cache performance with numerical examples.
content_points: H = hit ratio (fraction of accesses served from cache); M = 1 - H.
T = T_c * H + T_m * M; T_m = memory access time on a miss.
Extreme cases: H=100% → T = T_c (ideal); H=0% → T = T_m > T_p (worse than without cache).
Numerical example: T_c=2ns, T_p=10ns, T_m=11ns, H=95% → T=2.45ns (>4× speedup).
Cache line: a block of consecutive main-memory bytes fetched together; exploits spatial locality.
Replacement policy determines which line to evict when cache is full.
learning_objectives: Calculate average memory access time given hit ratio and access times
Explain the role of cache lines in exploiting spatial locality
